LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE PRACTICED LAW AT THE SAME LAW FIRM AS MR. FALK AND MR. MAYER - WHAT WAS THEN KNOWN AS HOWARD RICE - FROM DECEMBER 1981 TO MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992-1995. HOWARD RICE IS NOW PART OF ARNOLD & PORTER. IN JANUARY OF THIS YEAR, JUDGE MILLER WAS A GUEST AT THE RETIREMENT DINNER FOR MR. FALK THAT WAS HOSTED BY ARNOLD & PORTER, TO WHICH MANY CURRENT AND FORMER COLLEAGUES OF MR. FALK'S FROM THE OLD HOWARD RICE FIRM WERE INVITED. MR. FALK AND MR. MAYER WERE AT THAT DINNER. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CARMEL MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFFS TO FURNISH SECURITY IS DENIED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And/ Or Their Counsel Of Record; Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Reginald S. Leese In Support And Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: BARRT W. LEE, ESQ.and ANN M. HEIMBERGER, ESQ. for CP III Rincon Towers, Inc., defendant and moving party; LISA C. MCCURDY, ESQ. for Rincon EV Realty, LLC, plaintiff and opposing party.

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.

DEFENDANT CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order is denied. By this motion the court does not determine whether the documents identified in the privilege log are properly designated as privileged. However, the court finds that the privileges have not been waived. If moving party believes that specific documents contained in the privilege log are not privileged, a motion must be made to have the court determine that dispute. This order does not bar the deposition, but it does bar the use of the documents identified in the privilege log until further order of the court. The documents identified in the privilege log shall be returned to Plaintiffs until further order of the court. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Depositions to be taken on 9/16 and 9/17 or 9/20 and 9/21 and no later than 9/30/10. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
